title: oxalis niederleinii
intro: Oxalis niederleinii, also known as the Niederlein's wood-sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family, Oxalidaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the Andean region of South America, and its leaves, stems, and roots have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Oxalis niederleinii include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and support the immune system. Oxalis niederleinii is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be found in some traditional medicine formulas and supplements.
